spec_match.rationale: Calls getAllScreensMedia(), iterates getVideoTracks(), paints each into its own <video>, snapshots all videos to one canvas. The motivating multi-monitor UI from the chromestatus rationale (contact center / trading desk dashboard).

interactive.rationale: Capture / stop / layout selector / snapshot all functional. Snapshot canvas reads from <video> elements via drawImage. Genuine end-to-end demo.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature index promises a live tile dashboard with 2/3/4-column grids and a one-canvas snapshot — page delivers all three.

edge_cases.rationale: Outside an IWA the placeholder text shows the error name + message which is good. But: no handling for >4 screens (extra screens spill out of the layout), snapshot canvas is fixed 320x180 per tile so high-DPI / 4K screens get downscaled silently, and `drawImage` from a still-loading video throws into the `try { } catch {}` swallow — the snapshot may be all black with no warning.

references.rationale: See also only links to sibling concepts. No https://chromestatus.com/feature/6284029979525120 and no spec link — violates CLAUDE.md rule #3 self-heal id embed.

design_system.rationale: Most styles use CSS variables, BUT `ctx.fillStyle = '#0a0a0a'` inlines a raw hex in JS — should be a computed style read (`getComputedStyle(...).getPropertyValue('--text-black')`) or at minimum a documented exception. Also `<h3 style=...>` inlines display rules that already live in the design system.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com reference link
openQuestions.detail: Rule #3: every page needs the chromestatus.com/feature/6284029979525120 link so the slug-recovery script can re-identify this folder. Currently only sibling concept links present.

openQuestions.title: Read fill colour from CSS variables, not raw hex
openQuestions.detail: The snapshot canvas's background uses `'#0a0a0a'` directly. Read `getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).getPropertyValue('--text-black')` instead, otherwise this page silently disagrees with the design system if the variable is ever rethemed.

openQuestions.title: Make the snapshot react to high-DPI / many-screens
openQuestions.detail: Snapshot is hard-coded 320x180 per tile. On a 5-monitor 4K setup that's a tiny postage stamp per screen. Read the source video's `videoWidth`/`videoHeight` and scale accordingly, or expose a 'full resolution' toggle.

openQuestions.title: Warn when capture is attempted before videos are ready
openQuestions.detail: The snapshot try/catch swallows drawImage InvalidStateError silently when a track has not produced a frame yet — the visitor sees an all-black canvas with no explanation. Either delay until `loadeddata` fires per video, or surface the error in the UI.

summary: End-to-end mosaic that delivers the motivating use case. Held back by a missing chromestatus reference, one raw-hex regression in the snapshot canvas, and a couple of edge cases (resolution, ready-state) hidden behind a swallowed try/catch.
